data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Amazon uses a microservices architecture with each service focused on a particular business capability.

data.title: AWS Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Amazon runs its services on AWS infrastructure, ensuring flexibility, scalability, and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Utilization of AWS cloud services such as EC2, S3, and RDS.

data.title: Load Balancing and Auto-Scaling
data.description: Load balancers distribute incoming application traffic across multiple targets to ensure high availability.
data.technical_spec: Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) with Auto-Scaling Groups.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Amazon uses a global CDN to deliver content with low latency.
data.technical_spec: Amazon CloudFront integration for dynamic and static content delivery.

data.title: Database Management
data.description: Combination of NoSQL and SQL databases for handling different data types.
data.technical_spec: Amazon DynamoDB and Amazon RDS for diverse data requirements.
